title: Deployments and Services
learningObjectives: Create Deployments with replica counts and label selectors
Expose Deployments with Services (ClusterIP, NodePort, LoadBalancer)
Use kubectl to scale, update, and roll back Deployments

theory.content:
Deployments

A Deployment manages a ReplicaSet, which ensures a specified number of Pod replicas are running. Deployments enable rolling updates and rollbacks.

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: nginx-deployment
  namespace: default
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: nginx
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: nginx
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: nginx
          image: nginx:1.27
          ports:
            - containerPort: 80
          resources:
            requests:
              cpu: "100m"
              memory: "128Mi"

Rolling Updates

spec:
  strategy:
    type: RollingUpdate
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 1        # Create 1 extra Pod during update
      maxUnavailable: 0  # Keep all existing Pods available

Services

Services provide a stable IP and DNS name for a set of Pods, with load balancing.

apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: nginx-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: nginx          # Selects Pods with this label
  ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 80          # Service port
      targetPort: 80    # Pod port
  type: ClusterIP       # Only within cluster

Service Types

  • ClusterIP (default): Accessible only within the cluster
  • NodePort: Exposes on each Node's IP at a static port (30000-32767)
  • LoadBalancer: Creates an external load balancer (cloud providers)
  • ExternalName: Maps to an external DNS name

kubectl Deployment Commands

# Scale a deployment
kubectl scale deployment nginx-deployment --replicas=5

# Update image (triggers rolling update)
kubectl set image deployment/nginx-deployment nginx=nginx:1.28

# Check rollout status
kubectl rollout status deployment/nginx-deployment

# View rollout history
kubectl rollout history deployment/nginx-deployment

# Roll back to previous version
kubectl rollout undo deployment/nginx-deployment
